title: My favorite types of Cheese - Request

enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a viewer request about his favorite cheeses and thoughts on cheese in general. He shares preferences for expensive aged cheese, yellow cheddar, string cheese, Swiss, and goat cheese, while musing on cheese history as fermented milk. He digresses into discomfort about milk sourcing and curiosity about human cheese.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Loves it but eats it sparingly because it deserves to be savored alone, not masked by other ingredients
His all-time favorite; considers it classic and still his go-to at Subway
Likes it but speculates it tastes milder because it's 'missing an ingredient'
Likes it despite the strong smell
Believes Switzerland makes the best cheese and takes it seriously
Skeptical and somewhat grossed out due to animal welfare concerns and documentaries about contamination
Curious and would buy it; thinks disgust is cultural conditioning

I've seen some of the documentaries and that's just gross.
But, uh, and then you make the cheese out of it too.
So, I don't know.
It's, I don't know.
You just don't have to block that out of your mind.

What about humans?
